mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 7.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy recovery, which expands profit margins for cyclical equities and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth × HOUST growth) shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day SPX returns across three consecutive market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent core inflation exceeding 3.0 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL implies inflation > 3.0%, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the next 5 trading days regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarterly changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 confirms robust labor demand, which reduces recession risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient drops below 0.02 when PAYEMS growth exceeds the threshold, or forward returns are indistinguishable from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real consumer spending and a rising unemployment rate signals a demand shock that erodes corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling RSAFS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of historical episodes, or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index falling below 100 indicates severe capacity underutilization and weak business investment, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal's directional accuracy does not exceed 55% in out-of-sample testing.
